title: Pouteria brachyloba
taxonomy: Pouteria brachyloba is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It belongs to the genus Pouteria, which comprises around 40 species of trees and shrubs. Pouteria brachyloba is native to Central and South America, where it grows in tropical forests and woodlands. The plant is classified under the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Pouteria brachyloba is also known by its common names, including Brazilian cherry and Pouteria.
uses: Pouteria brachyloba, also known as the sabine or sabine tree, is a plant species native to the Americas. Pouteria brachyloba has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its bark, leaves, and roots are often used to prepare medicinal remedies.
